original_abstract: Abstract Bayesian Networks (BNs) have become increasingly popular over the last few decades as a tool for reasoning under uncertainty in fields as diverse as medicine, biology, epidemiology, economics and the social sciences. This is especially true in real-world areas where we seek to answer complex questions based on hypothetical evidence to determine actions for intervention. However, determining the graphical structure of a BN remains a major challenge, especially when modelling a problem under causal assumptions. Solutions to this problem include the automated discovery of BN graphs from data, constructing them based on expert knowledge, or a combination of the two. This paper provides a comprehensive review of combinatoric algorithms proposed for learning BN structure from data, describing 74 algorithms including prototypical, well-established and state-of-the-art approaches. The basic approach of each algorithm is described in consistent terms, and the similarities and differences between them highlighted. Methods of evaluating algorithms and their comparative performance are discussed including the consistency of claims made in the literature. Approaches for dealing with data noise in real-world datasets and incorporating expert knowledge into the learning process are also covered.
humanized_abstract: Abstract Bayesian Networks (BNs) have been widely applied to various fields for uncertainty reasoning over the last few decades. As many complex real-world problems need to investigate the effects of hypothetical interventions or decisions, BNs have emerged as a powerful tool to model, analyze and manage complex systems. While BNs are a very valuable representation of uncertain knowledge, one major open issue is how to determine a suitable graph structure for a model when one has some prior causal belief on the relationships between variables. Therefore, many approaches have been designed in order to automatically deduce a graph from data, to construct a BN from prior knowledge, or even a combination of these two approaches. This paper gives a survey on the combinatoric algorithms dedicated to learning BN structures from data, including 74 algorithms that are exemplified, well-known and that belong to the state-of-the-art on this topic. We describe for each of them their main procedures in a unified presentation framework, which enables us to highlight their common or differing aspects. We also study how these works evaluate the performance of the algorithms they propose and how reliability is ensured when reporting on such comparisons, especially in presence of high noisiness in the data or when incorporating prior knowledge.
